title: Syracuse lawmakers approve sale of former fire station where apartments, eatery planned
summary: Syracuse Common Council approved the $900,000 sale of shuttered Fire Station 7 to JF Real Estate for a $2.5M mixed-use redevelopment (ground-floor restaurant, 4 apartments). The project advances the city's Near East Side corridor redevelopment strategy tied to I-81 viaduct removal, with closing expected in 9-12 months and construction in ~12 months. Fire Department identified alternative University Avenue parcels for future station relocation.

political_signal.description: Administration advancing strategic I-81 corridor redevelopment with stakeholder buy-in (residents, fire department); demonstrates execution on downtown-to-University Hill connectivity vision.

processing_notes: Station 7 closure date 2013 provides historical context; $2.5M project cost indicates significant private capital investment; timeline (9-12 mo closing + ~12 mo construction) extends impact visibility through 2026-2027; Welch Terrace resident approval signals constituent pressure mitigation; fire relocation strategy documented but contingent on future University Avenue acquisition
